column1: 2;Best;7/5/2024;HI Comp @ AAMAS 2024;MarkBaz;T1. Research HI Team;"In a bustling pharmaceutical laboratory
column2: a team of researchers gathered around a conference table
column3: their faces illuminated by the glow of computer screens displaying complex molecular structures and scientific literature. Among them stood Dr. Emily Chen
column4: a seasoned neuroscientist leading the charge in the company's quest to combat neurodegeneration
column6: AlphaFold enabling simulations of protein behaviour that would otherwise require days or weeks of assistants for each protein. Having had extensive training in how AlphaFold works
column7: Emily and her AI assistant have a high level of Autonomy and Team Structure Transparency enabling independence of team members to explore different aspects of the problem.

column1: The team's objective was clear: to identify proteins with the potential to inhibit neurodegeneration. Dr. Chen turned to AlphaFold
column2: the revolutionary AI system renowned for its unparalleled accuracy in predicting protein structures. With AlphaFold's assistance
column3: the team aimed to analyse the intricate folds and binding sites of proteins implicated in neurodegenerative disorders such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases. Whilst Emily and her AI team have a high level of mutual dependency
column4: she is able to maximise her time usage by reducing the time taken to fold proteins and maximise her research output. Her team extends to IT research support staff
column5: who meet weekly over video call to discuss how her use of AlphaFold can be maximised. Recently
column6: they have developed a new sensor for her lab which can directly communicate with AlphaFold
column7: developed purely due to the effective communication between the team. In these weekly meetings
column8: team processes are discussed and rigorously criticised with the intention of finding optimisations that may lead to breakthroughs. The high quality of the group process has led to less time wasted on false-positive experiments.

column1: As Dr. Chen fed protein sequences into AlphaFold's neural network
column2: the AI swiftly generated high-fidelity 3D models of their structures
column3: unveiling hidden intricacies invisible to the human eye. With each prediction
column4: AlphaFold provided valuable insights into the structural dynamics of the proteins
column5: highlighting potential sites for therapeutic intervention. As Dr. Chen is confident in the quality of AlphaFoldâ€™s task performance; it gives her greater freedom to spend her time on the proteins that present the best results. However
column6: Dr. Chen is also aware of the weaknesses of AlphaFold
column7: and submits her experiments earlier in the day to ensure they are completed before the end of the day.

column1: Back at Dr. Chen's workstation
column2: AlphaFold unveiled a protein structure with a unique binding pocket predicted to modulate neurodegenerative pathways. Excitement rippled through the laboratory as the team recognized the potential significance of their discovery. Armed with this newfound insight
column3: Dr. Chen and her colleagues delved deeper into the literature
column4: cross-referencing AlphaFold's predictions with a large language model's summaries to uncover additional evidence supporting the protein's role in neuroprotection.";T4. Cybersecurity Response Team;"Following months of increased denial-of-service attacks from bad actors external to our organisation
column5: a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability has allowed the balance to swing into the attackers favour. To save costs in this organisation
column6: jobs requiring AI inference are performed in the cloud.

column1: A large degree of complacency is apparent in our organisation
column2: and no contingency planning has been done despite the continuing denial-of-service attacks. The team is heavily independent
column3: and humans rely significantly on the AI to identify which attacks require attention first. Furthermore
column4: the humans work from multiple offices across different time zones
column5: utilising an internal tool for communication which utilises the internet.

column1: At the onset of the attack
column2: the attack partially removes the ability for the organisation to access the internet
column3: removing the AI agents to help in the problem as they are run on external servers and for our humans to communicate between offices. This reduces the team's skill comprehensiveness
column4: as human agents have not been trained to complete the jobs that the AI agents are unable to do
column5: and the humans are not able to communicate to describe which tasks they are working on
column6: wasting precious time when members are unknowingly working on the same problem.

column1: The humans are able to partially restore the AI
column2: but the zero-day vulnerability is one which the AI agents have not been exposed to before. This out of distribution attack means that their inferred best actions are no longer the correct decision
column3: and to reduce their exposure
column4: the human members are required to shadow the agent's actions. As the human members are required to cover where the AI agents are failing to perform
column5: they quickly become overwhelmed due to the increasing workload. This leads to frustration and a breakdown of team communication
column6: further reducing their effectiveness and efficiency. Mistakes quickly lead to an even more increased workload

column1: After a period when the attack is finally mitigated
column2: the dust settles to reveal that customer and employee data has been stolen
column3: leading to legal liabilities for our organisation."

column1: On the other hand

column3: and instead of orienting her interests she adopted a behaviourist approach (Wheldall & Merrett

column1: The presence of a child in the team is

column1: Last
column2: we must consider the socio-economic factor: the intervention takes place in a specific context
column3: which is that of a school in a wealthy area of the city. Since the intervention aims to help children with learning development
column4: account should be taken of the fact that children raised in poor families show this type of problem most (Bigelow
column5: 2006). An intervention designed for a particular social class is difficult to extend in a situation where the possibilities of families are limited and the effects on children more serious (Objectives consequentiality)."
